text: This study investigates the structural evolution and luminescence enhancement of (Sr,Ca)AlSiN₃:Eu²⁺ phosphors achieved through systematic cation substitution. By replacing Ca²⁺ with larger Sr²⁺ ions, the host lattice undergoes a selective anisotropic expansion; while the lattice volume and b-c plane dimensions increase, a contraction is observed in the interlayer distance along the a-axis. This structural modification induces a significant distortion of the AlSiN₃ framework, creating high-coordination environments for the Sr/Eu centers. X-ray absorption near-edge structure (XANES) spectroscopy reveals that these local geometric changes facilitate favorable charge variations in the europium activators when synthesized under a reducing atmosphere. Consequently, the modified phosphors exhibit a blue shift in emission and a performance enhancement of over 10% in photoluminescent intensity. The correlation between macroscopic lattice parameters, local coordination environments, and electronic transitions provides a detailed framework for optimizing nitride-based phosphors for high-efficiency white light-emitting diode (wLED) applications.
